design_philosophy.core_loop: Menu-based turn combat with Active Time Battle system; exploration of overworld/towns/dungeons; character progression through experience and equipment; story progression through narrative events
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Rebellion against an immoral military dictatorship; magical arms race; personal redemption arcs against systemic oppression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Leading an expanding cast of fourteen playable characters through an industrial-fantasy world with personal stories; tactical party-based combat; discovering hidden treasures and character abilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Darker, more mature storytelling than earlier Final Fantasy entries; thematic focus on consequences of war, chemical weapons, violent apocalyptic confrontations, teenage pregnancy, and societal collapse; first mainline FF directed by someone other than Sakaguchi; ensemble cast approach rather than single protagonist

difficulty.checkpointing: Savepoints at towns and key locations within dungeons
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character customization through equipment, magic learning, and ability selection; party composition choices; optional side-quests and hidden treasures

progression.upgrade_system: Experience points and levels; equipment acquisition through shops and dungeon treasures; magic learning; character-specific unique special abilities
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story progression gates access to new areas; later in game side-quests become available in certain towns; multiple playable characters unlock throughout narrative
replayability_hooks: Fourteen permanent playable characters with unique abilities to explore
Hidden treasures in dungeons not available in stores
Optional side-quests activated by visiting towns at specific story points
Character customization and party composition choices affecting playstyle

world_building_techniques: Industrial Revolution-era setting with visible technological advancement
Town NPCs offering contextual information and lore
Environmental storytelling through dungeon design (caves, forests, buildings with narrative purpose)
Story-driven overworld progression unlocking new areas and context
Character-driven narrative with personal redemption arcs

faction_indicators: Characters with conflicting loyalties and redemption arcs
environmental_storytelling: Industrial technology level reflected in world design
Dungeon variety (caves, forests, buildings) suggesting different regions and purposes
Towns as centers of commerce and information reflecting social structure
